mechanism_narrative: Positive momentum in retail sales signals improving near-term household demand and upward revisions to corporate revenue expectations, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days when measured as the latest RSAFS growth rate.
evidence_refs: series:RSAFS
fact:RSAFS latest value 407805.0 as of t-1mo
failure_condition: Reject if higher RSAFS 3-month growth does not produce higher subsequent 20-day S&P 500 returns in walk-forward tests, with Rank IC below 0.03 over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A crossing of unemployment above a high-stress threshold signals deteriorating labor income and rising recession risk, reducing expected earnings and producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: series:UNRATE
fact:UNRATE latest value 8.3 as of t-1mo
failure_condition: Reject if triggered observations following UNRATE crossings above 8.0% have positive 20-day S&P 500 returns in more than 60% of cases or average triggered returns are not below unconditional returns.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate is pinned near zero while unemployment is elevated, monetary accommodation offsets part of the labor shock by lowering discount rates, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS crosses below 0.25% while UNRATE is above 7.5%
evidence_refs: series:FEDFUNDS
series:UNRATE
fact:FEDFUNDS latest value 0.1 as of t-1mo
fact:UNRATE latest value 8.3 as of t-1mo
failure_condition: Reject if the joint FEDFUNDS below 0.25% and UNRATE above 7.5% state is followed by negative average 60-day S&P 500 returns or does not outperform high-unemployment periods without near-zero rates.

mechanism_narrative: A decline in industrial production signals weakening cyclical output and pressure on operating leverage, leading investors to mark down earnings expectations and generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: series:INDPRO
fact:INDPRO latest value 95.9054 as of t-2mo
failure_condition: Reject if INDPRO 3-month contractions are not followed by lower 20-day S&P 500 returns than expansions, or if the sign of the walk-forward coefficient is positive in most rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts below a depressed activity threshold signal weak construction demand and tighter household balance-sheet conditions, reducing confidence in cyclical earnings and producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
evidence_refs: series:HOUST
fact:HOUST latest value 699.0 as of t-2mo
failure_condition: Reject if HOUST readings below 750 thousand units are followed by positive average 60-day S&P 500 returns and do not underperform periods with HOUST above 750 thousand units.

mechanism_narrative: Rising consumer prices combined with a near-zero policy rate signals a negative real-rate inflation shock that erodes margins and raises future tightening risk, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL 6-month annualized growth crosses above 3% while FEDFUNDS is below 0.25%
evidence_refs: series:CPIAUCSL
series:FEDFUNDS
fact:CPIAUCSL latest value 227.505 as of t-2mo
fact:FEDFUNDS latest value 0.1 as of t-1mo
failure_condition: Reject if the high-CPI-growth and near-zero-FEDFUNDS interaction is followed by nonnegative average 20-day S&P 500 returns or if CPI growth has no incremental negative effect when FEDFUNDS is below 0.25%.

mechanism_narrative: A positive payroll growth breakout signals improving labor income and reduced default risk, supporting near-term risk appetite and producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS 1-month change crosses above 150 thousand jobs
evidence_refs: series:PAYEMS
fact:PAYEMS latest value 132697.0 as of t-1mo
failure_condition: Reject if PAYEMS increases above 150 thousand jobs are followed by lower average 5-day S&P 500 returns than non-triggered months or if hit rate is below 50% after transaction-day alignment.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real GDP and industrial production confirms broad output recovery rather than inventory noise, raising expected earnings growth and producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 quarter-over-quarter growth crosses above 0% while INDPRO 3-month growth is above 0%
evidence_refs: series:GDPC1
series:INDPRO
fact:GDPC1 latest value 13429.9 as of t-5mo
fact:INDPRO latest value 95.9054 as of t-2mo
failure_condition: Reject if the joint GDPC1 and INDPRO expansion state fails to deliver positive average 60-day S&P 500 returns or does not outperform periods where only one of the two output proxies is expanding.
